dimension: CURRENT Bridge types (working tree) and the backend-API gap vs the Swaps API v2 pattern

files_read.role: Swaps API v2 + Config API v2 backend wire contract (the pattern to mirror). 922 lines. Contains ZERO bridge endpoints/DTOs.
Declares BridgeLimit type and SpokeExecActionParams/WalletProviderSlot wrappers used by BridgeParams.
SDK BridgeService: defines CreateBridgeIntentParams, BridgeParams, BridgeServiceConstructorParams and all public bridge methods. 850 lines.
Bridge error code unions, error types, and type guards.
Comparison reference: swap config + static swapSupportedTokens lists (bridge has no equivalent static list).
Backend barrel: re-exports backendApi.js (v1) + backendApiV2.js. v1 has zero bridge refs.

key_findings: CRITICAL GAP: backendApiV2.ts (working tree, 922 lines) declares NO bridge API endpoints or DTOs. Its two client interfaces are ISwapsApiV2 (22 methods: getTokens, getTokensByChain, getQuote, getDeadline, checkAllowance, approve, createIntent, submitIntent, getStatus, cancelIntent, getIntentHash, getSolvedIntentPacket, getIntentSubmitTxExtraData, getFilledIntent, getIntent, createLimitOrderIntent, estimateGas, getPartnerFee, getSolverFee, submitTx, getSubmitTxStatus) and IConfigApiV2 (8 methods). The word 'bridge' appears only in DOCSTRINGS (line 108 'bridged liquidity' on SwapTokenV2.vault; lines 781/783 listing BridgeDefaultConfig as a reused config field). v1 backendApi.ts has ZERO bridge references.

CONFIRMED on reference branch: origin/feat/swaps-api-v2's backendApiV2.ts ALSO adds no bridge types (diff vs base touches only swap/config; grep for added 'bridge' lines is empty). So the Swaps API v2 work is purely swaps+config; the Bridge API must add an entirely NEW parallel bridge backend contract from scratch following the same conventions.

@sodax/types bridge types that EXIST today (exhaustive): (1) BridgeLimit in common/common.ts:413 = `{ amount: bigint; decimals: number; type: 'DEPOSIT_LIMIT' | 'WITHDRAWAL_LIMIT' }`. (2) sodax-config/sodax-config.ts: `BridgeConfig = Prettify<BridgeDefaultConfig & BridgeOptions>`, `BridgeOptions = { partnerFee?: PartnerFee }`, `BridgeDefaultConfig = {}` (empty, comment 'kept for future extension'), `const bridgeConfig = {} satisfies BridgeDefaultConfig`. (3) `bridge?: BridgeOptions` on SodaxOptionalConfig (line 72), `bridge: BridgeDefaultConfig` on SodaxDefaultConfig (line 95), `bridge: bridgeConfig` in sodaxConfig (line 111). (4) `'bridge'` is a member of SodaxFeature (shared/features.ts:12).

Bridge param types live in the SDK (@sodax/sdk bridge module), NOT in @sodax/types. In packages/sdk/src/bridge/BridgeService.ts:63-77: `export type CreateBridgeIntentParams<K extends SpokeChainKey = SpokeChainKey> = { srcAddress: string; srcChainKey: K; srcToken: string; amount: bigint; dstChainKey: SpokeChainKey; dstToken: string; recipient: string }` (recipient is non-encoded). And `export type BridgeParams<ChainKey extends SpokeChainKey, Raw extends boolean> = SpokeExecActionParams<ChainKey, Raw, CreateBridgeIntentParams<ChainKey>>`.

SpokeExecActionParams<K, Raw, P, E=never> (common/common.ts:435) = `{ params: P; extras?: E; skipSimulation?: boolean; timeout?: number } & WalletProviderSlot<K, Raw>`. WalletProviderSlot<K,Raw> (line 421): Raw=true => `{ raw: true; walletProvider?: never }`; Raw=false => `{ raw?: false; walletProvider: GetWalletProviderType<K> }`. This is the raw/unsigned vs signed split — backend uses raw:true to get unsigned tx; frontend uses raw:false with a wallet provider.

BridgeService public method signatures (packages/sdk/src/bridge/BridgeService.ts): `getFee(inputAmount: bigint): bigint`; `isAllowanceValid<S,Raw>(_params: BridgeParams<S,Raw>): Promise<Result<boolean, BridgeAllowanceCheckError>>`; `approve<K,Raw>(_params: BridgeParams<K,Raw>): Promise<Result<TxReturnType<K,Raw>, BridgeApproveError>>`; `bridge<K>(_params: BridgeParams<K,false>): Promise<Result<TxHashPair, BridgeOrchestrationError>>` returns `{ srcChainTxHash, dstChainTxHash }`; `createBridgeIntent<K,Raw>(_params: BridgeParams<K,Raw>): Promise<Result<IntentTxResult<K,Raw>, BridgeCreateIntentError>>` returns `{ tx, relayData }`; `getBridgeableAmount(from: XToken, to: XToken): Promise<Result<BridgeLimit, BridgeLookupError>>`; `isBridgeable({from, to, unchecked?}): boolean`; `getBridgeableTokens(from: SpokeChainKey, to: SpokeChainKey, token: string): Result<XToken[], BridgeLookupError>`; `filterTokensWithSameVault(tokens: Record<string, XToken>, to: SpokeChainKey, srcToken: XToken | undefined): XToken[]`; `findTokenBalanceInReserves(reserves: VaultReserves, token: XToken): bigint`.

Bridge has NO static supported-tokens list (unlike swap's swapSupportedTokens / stagingSwapSupportedTokens which are `Record<SpokeChainKey, readonly XToken[]>`). Bridgeability is COMPUTED at runtime: two tokens are bridgeable iff they resolve to the SAME hub vault address on Sonic (`srcToken.vault.toLowerCase() === dstToken.vault.toLowerCase()`). Source of truth is `config.spokeChainConfig[chainKey].supportedTokens` (a `Record<string, XToken>`). A Bridge API /tokens endpoint must derive bridgeable tokens via vault matching, not a static list.

The bridge IS already represented in Config API v2: SodaxConfigV2 (backendApiV2.ts:790) reuses SodaxDefaultConfig untouched for the `bridge` field, so GetAllConfigResponseV2.config.bridge is served — but because `BridgeDefaultConfig = {}` it carries no meaningful data on the wire today.

Backend submit-tx + fallback mechanism is SWAP-ONLY today and does NOT exist for bridge. The current bridge() does CLIENT-SIDE relay via `relayTxAndWaitPacket({ srcTxHash, data: relayData, chainKey, relayerApiEndpoint, timeout })` inside BridgeService.bridge() (lines 358-365). The Swaps API v2 backend submit-tx contract (SubmitTxRequestV2 {txHash, srcChainKey, walletAddress, intent: IntentRequestV2, relayData: string}, SubmitTxResponseV2 {success, data:{status:'inserted'|'duplicate', message}}, SubmitTxStatusQueryV2, SubmitTxStatusResponseV2 with SubmitSwapTxStatusV2 lifecycle 'pending'|'relaying'|'relayed'|'posting_execution'|'executed'|'failed') has NO bridge analog. There is NO `useBackendSubmitTx` hook anywhere in the working tree; the swaps-api-v2 branch implements this as packages/dapp-kit/src/hooks/swapsApi/useSwapsApiSubmitTx.ts and useSwapsApiSubmitTxStatus.ts (bridge would need parallel hooks).

Bridge error infrastructure (packages/sdk/src/bridge/errors.ts) already exists and is reusable: BridgeOrchestrationError, BridgeCreateIntentError, BridgeApproveError, BridgeAllowanceCheckError, BridgeLookupError, BridgeError plus guards (isBridgeOrchestrationError, isBridgeCreateIntentError, etc.). Single user action 'bridge'; read-only methods emit LOOKUP_FAILED partitioned by context.method ('getBridgeableAmount', 'getBridgeableTokens').

JSON-safety conventions any new bridge wire DTOs MUST follow (documented in backendApiV2.ts header lines 1-31): outbound RESPONSE types are pure JSON (every bigint -> decimal string, every Date -> ISO string, never bigint); inbound REQUEST types may mirror parsed DTOs where the Intent struct carries bigint (IntentRequestV2) while other amounts/deadlines are decimal strings; Hex/Address/Hash/SpokeChainKey are plain string; chain-specific opaque payloads (unsigned tx, gas, relay result) are `unknown`. Request/response shapes are split into *RequestV2 / *ResponseV2 pairs. A compile-time guard (`_ContainsBigint<T>` + `_AssertJsonSafe<Ok>`, lines 903-922) is intersected onto exported response/request types (e.g. GetAllConfigResponseV2, CreateLimitOrderParamsV2) to fail the build if bigint leaks into a JSON-safe type.

dapp-kit bridge hooks already consume the SDK types (packages/dapp-kit/src/hooks/bridge/): useBridge.ts (`UseBridgeVars<K> = Omit<BridgeParams<K,false>,'raw'>`, returns TxHashPair), useBridgeApprove.ts (`UseBridgeApproveVars<K> = Omit<BridgeParams<K,false>,'raw'>`), useBridgeAllowance.ts (payload: `CreateBridgeIntentParams<K> | undefined`), useGetBridgeableTokens.ts (calls `sodax.bridge.getBridgeableTokens(from, to, token)`). These import from '@sodax/sdk'. They are direct-SDK (on-chain) hooks, not backend-API hooks.
open_questions: Will the Bridge API reuse the SDK's CreateBridgeIntentParams shape (srcAddress/srcChainKey/srcToken/amount/dstChainKey/dstToken/recipient) projected to JSON (amount bigint -> decimal string) as its wire create-intent DTO? Swaps used a divergent CreateIntentParamsV2 (inputToken/outputToken/minOutputAmount/deadline/allowPartialFill/solver/data) rather than the SDK SwapParams shape, so bridge may similarly diverge.

Does bridge have an on-chain 'intent struct' analogous to IntentRequestV2/IntentResponseV2? Bridge createBridgeIntent returns IntentTxResult `{ tx, relayData }` (no hub Intent struct in the SDK return), so a Bridge API may not need an Intent DTO and submit-tx may carry relayData (hub address + payload) instead of an intent struct.

Should the Bridge API expose getBridgeableTokens / getBridgeableAmount as backend endpoints (they require on-chain reads: EvmVaultTokenService.getTokenInfos + getVaultReserves against the hub publicClient), or stay client-side? Swaps moved equivalent reads server-side; bridge's vault/reserve reads are good candidates for backend endpoints.

BridgeDefaultConfig is currently empty `{}`. If the Bridge API needs a server-served bridge config slice (e.g. supported/bridgeable token map per chain), decide whether to populate BridgeDefaultConfig or add a dedicated /config/bridge/... endpoint to IConfigApiV2 (mirroring GetSwapTokensConfigResponseV2).

Confirm naming: swaps used the route prefix /swaps/* and DTO suffix *V2 with interface ISwapsApiV2. Bridge should presumably use /bridge/* and IBridgeApiV2 — verify against issue #255 / PR #210 before scaffolding.
replication_recipe: Add a new Bridge API v2 section to packages/types/src/backend/backendApiV2.ts (or a sibling file re-exported via backend/index.ts) mirroring the Swaps section structure: one *RequestV2/*ResponseV2 type per endpoint, plain primitives, bigint->decimal string on responses, `unknown` for chain-specific tx/relay payloads.

Define a bridge token DTO mirroring SwapTokenV2 (symbol/name/decimals/address/chainKey/hubAsset/vault) e.g. BridgeTokenV2 — or reuse SwapTokenV2/XToken — and response types: GetBridgeableTokensResponseV2 (likely keyed by destination chain, or `readonly XToken[]` from getBridgeableTokens), plus a GetBridgeableAmountResponseV2 projecting BridgeLimit `{ amount, decimals, type }` with amount as decimal string.

Define bridge create-intent request/response DTOs: a JSON-safe projection of CreateBridgeIntentParams (srcAddress, srcChainKey, srcToken, amount as decimal string, dstChainKey, dstToken, recipient) -> CreateBridgeIntentParamsV2, and a response mirroring the SDK IntentTxResult `{ tx: unknown; relayData: RelayExtraDataResponseV2 }` (reuse the existing RelayExtraDataRequestV2/ResponseV2 {address, payload}).

Add bridge allowance/approve/gas/fee DTOs by reusing the swap patterns: AllowanceCheckResponseV2 {valid}, ApproveResponseV2 {tx:unknown}, GasEstimateRequestV2/ResponseV2, FeeQueryV2/FeeResponseV2 (bridge getFee is partner-fee only -> a getBridgePartnerFee endpoint).

Add the backend submit-tx + status contract for bridge (the central mechanism), mirroring SubmitTxRequestV2/SubmitTxResponseV2/SubmitTxStatusQueryV2/SubmitTxStatusResponseV2 and the lifecycle enums (SubmitSwapTxStatusV2 -> a SubmitBridgeTxStatusV2, PacketDataV2/PacketDataStatusV2 reusable). The request likely carries {txHash, srcChainKey, walletAddress, relayData} (and possibly no intent struct, since bridge createBridgeIntent has no hub Intent struct — verify).

Declare an aggregating IBridgeApiV2 client interface (one async method per endpoint, post-serialization wire shapes) parallel to ISwapsApiV2, e.g. getBridgeableTokens, getBridgeableAmount, checkAllowance, approve, createBridgeIntent, submitTx, getSubmitTxStatus, estimateGas, getPartnerFee. Do NOT `implements` it on the NestJS controller (same note as ISwapsApiV2).

Apply the JSON-safety compile-time guard: intersect `& _AssertJsonSafe<[_ContainsBigint<T>] extends [false] ? true : false>` onto at least one exported bridge request and response `type` (reuse the existing _ContainsBigint/_AssertJsonSafe helpers at the bottom of backendApiV2.ts) so a leaked bigint fails the build.

In @sodax/dapp-kit, add bridge backend-API hooks paralleling the swaps-api-v2 branch's packages/dapp-kit/src/hooks/swapsApi/useSwapsApiSubmitTx.ts + useSwapsApiSubmitTxStatus.ts: e.g. useBridgeApiSubmitTx + useBridgeApiSubmitTxStatus, that POST the raw/unsigned tx (created via SDK raw:true) result + relayData to the backend submit-tx endpoint and poll status, with the existing client-side relayTxAndWaitPacket as the fallback path.

Decide whether to populate BridgeDefaultConfig / add a /config/bridge slice to IConfigApiV2 vs keeping bridge config empty; if bridgeable tokens become a config endpoint, mirror GetSwapTokensConfigResponseV2 = Record<string, readonly XToken[]>.

Keep bridge param/orchestration logic in @sodax/sdk (BridgeService) and only add JSON-safe wire DTOs to @sodax/types — preserve the dependency direction (@sodax/types has zero runtime deps; do not import viem/SDK branded types — project to plain string).
